Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to understand the DECLARE model and its constraints The model is given as a Python dictionary with keys init existence responded existence precedence noncoexistence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The noncoexistence constraint between E and C contradicts the existence of C and the natural flow that includes both E and C

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: existence C requires C to appear noncoexistence E C forbids C and E to appear together responded existence if A then E requires that if A occurs then E appears which then conflicts with noncoexistence because existence C requires C

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Misinterpretation of business requirements The analyst might have misunderstood the relationship between evaluation and closing

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Incremental changes The process might have been updated over time originally evaluation was required but later a shortcut was introduced to close without evaluation and the model was not updated consistently

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Technical issues The constraints were automatically derived from data using some algorithm that produced false positives or contradictory rules due to noise or methodological errors

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Pressure to handle claims quickly Under pressure the organization might allow skipping evaluation for certain claims but the model still enforces evaluation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can list a few hypotheses

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check for claims that have both Evaluate E and Close C coexistence violation Also claims that have A but no E violating responded existence Claims that have C but no R violating precedence Claims that have no R violating init Claims that have no C violating existence

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The model constraints have support and confidence 1 0 meaning they are supposed to be 100 percent true So any deviation indicates an anomaly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check for claims missing required start activity R

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check for claims missing required end activity C

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check for claims where C occurs without a preceding R precedence violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check for coexistence of E and C noncoexistence violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check for Assign A without subsequent Evaluate E responded existence violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check if evaluation steps are performed by assigned adjusters and whether the adjuster specialization matches the claim type
